description: Fix contributing documentation inaccuracies: correct misleading browser-extension publishConfig claim and add missing npm rebuild build pipeline step.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix misleading browser-extension publishConfig claim in publishing.mdx
userStories.description: As a platform contributor reading the publishing guide, I want the publishConfig description to be accurate so that I understand which packages are restricted and why.
acceptanceCriteria: The sentence at docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx line 99 no longer claims browser-extension is an exception to the restricted publishConfig pattern
The replacement text accurately states that all seven packages use restricted access (six via explicit publishConfig, browser-extension via npm's default for scoped packages)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx (line 99)

Current text:
"All packages except `browser-extension` use `"publishConfig": { "access": "restricted" }` — only `@opentabs-dev` org members can install them."

This is misleading. In reality:
- Six packages (shared, mcp-server, plugin-sdk, plugin-tools, cli, create-plugin) have explicit `"publishConfig": { "access": "restricted" }` in their package.json
- browser-extension has NO publishConfig field at all
- Since browser-extension is a scoped package (@opentabs-dev/browser-extension), npm defaults to restricted access for scoped packages
- So ALL seven packages are effectively restricted — browser-extension is NOT an exception

Replace with something like:
"Six packages use `"publishConfig": { "access": "restricted" }` explicitly. `browser-extension` omits `publishConfig` but is still restricted — npm defaults to restricted access for scoped packages. Only `@opentabs-dev` org members can install any of them."

Keep it concise — one or two sentences max.

userStories.title: Add missing npm rebuild step to dev-setup build pipeline
userStories.description: As a platform contributor reading the dev setup guide, I want the build pipeline description to include all steps so that I understand what npm run build actually does.
acceptanceCriteria: The build pipeline steps list in docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx includes a 6th step describing the npm rebuild for CLI and plugin-tools
The step description mentions that it rebuilds @opentabs-dev/cli and @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools to link binaries after TypeScript compilation
The numbered list now has 6 items instead of 5
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx (lines 31-37)

The build pipeline description lists 5 steps:
1. tsc --build
2. Extension bundle
3. Side panel
4. Icon generation
5. Extension install

But the actual build script in root package.json has a 6th step at the end:
`npm rebuild @opentabs-dev/cli @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools`

Full build script from package.json:
"build": "tsc --build && npm run --prefix platform/browser-extension build:bundle && npm run --prefix platform/browser-extension build:side-panel && tsx scripts/generate-icons.ts && tsx scripts/install-extension.ts && npm rebuild @opentabs-dev/cli @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools"

Add step 6: `npm rebuild` — rebuilds the CLI and plugin-tools packages to ensure their bin scripts are properly linked after TypeScript compilation. This is necessary because npm workspaces link binaries from dist/ during install, but after tsc --build regenerates dist/ files, the bin links may be stale.

Keep the description concise and follow the same format as the other steps (numbered item with em-dash explanation).
